---
title: "Research"
description: "Research interests and current work by Lukas A. Grahl."
---

My research lies at the intersection of **household finance** and **behavioural finance**. I study how investors make decisions under uncertainty and ambiguity, how attention and personal experience shape trading, and how information is incorporated into prices around important market events.

## Research interests

- Household finance and retail investor behaviour
- Decision-making under ambiguity and uncertainty
- Belief formation, probability weighting, and investor attention
- Empirical asset pricing and information processing

## Working papers

Working papers and drafts will be added here as they become publicly available.

<!--
To add a working paper, copy and edit this block:

### Paper title

**With Coauthor Name**  
*Working paper, Month Year*

Two or three sentences describing the research question, method, and main contribution.

[Paper](files/paper-name.pdf) · [Slides](files/slides-name.pdf) · [Code](https://github.com/...)

---
-->

## Work in progress

A selection of current projects examines revealed preferences from household portfolios, retail trading around earnings announcements, and the empirical distinction between distorted beliefs and non-standard preferences.

<!-- Remove or revise the sentence above if you prefer not to describe work in progress publicly. -->
